title: Conversational failures evaporate unless Ash hand-pastes logs - mine correction/frustration signals from turns.jsonl into candidate tickets
source: Claude Fable improvement pass 2026-07-07 - the scholarship failure ('AGAIN why are u showing me women shyt', 'And I'm an F1 studnet? What's wrong with you') recurred precisely because the first occurrence never became a ticket
current_state: Pi logs every turn to logs/turns.jsonl, and scripts/passive/ticket_candidate_miner.py already scans the last 200 turns (source 5, MAX_LOG_TURNS=200) - but only for 'repeated patterns', not for the highest-signal failure evidence that exists: the user CORRECTING or getting frustrated at Pi. Tonight's conversation contained 'Again why are u showing me...', 'What's wrong with you?', and an explicit factual correction ('I'm an F1 student') - none of which produced a candidate ticket. The analysis-pipeline (analysis/WORKFLOW.md) catches these only when Ash manually pastes conversations, which he almost never does. Code failures auto-ticket; conversation failures rely on human clerical work.
target_state: The miner's turns.jsonl source also flags correction/frustration signals in user-role turns: repeated-complaint markers ('again', 'i already told you', 'i told you'), correction openers ('no,', 'wrong', "that's not", 'actually i'), exasperation ('what's wrong with you', 'wtf', 'bruh', 'donkey'). Each hit emits a candidate row (existing analysis/candidate_tickets.jsonl schema, new source tag 'correction_signal') carrying the triggering user turn plus the preceding assistant turn (truncated ~300 chars each) so triage has context. Dedup: one candidate per (signal, day). Tests with synthetic turns.jsonl fixtures: a correction sequence yields exactly one candidate with both turns quoted; a benign 'again' in normal prose ('see you again tomorrow') does NOT fire (patterns anchor to turn-initial position or direct-address forms).
migration_plan: 1. Read the miner's existing source-5 turn scan + candidate emission helpers - extend, do not fork; reuse its dedup and append machinery.

2. Tests first (extend testing/test_ticket_candidate_miner.py... check actual test filename via grep; the T-252 regex-bug fix added tests there): tmp turns.jsonl with (a) assistant reply -> user 'no, I'm an F-1 student' -> one candidate, both turns excerpted; (b) user 'can't wait to see you again' -> zero candidates; (c) same correction twice in one day -> one candidate.

3. Add the pattern set as a module constant with word-boundary regexes anchored to turn start or direct address ('why are you/u', "what's wrong with you") - learn from the T-252 lesson (the 'HACK inside Hacker News' bug: always use word boundaries).
4. python scripts/verify.py must print PASS (never pipe, T-214); SOLUTIONS.jsonl; close; refresh_pi.py.
risk_notes: scripts/passive/ only - SAFE component, verified, reproducing tests specified: a genuine sprint.py candidate. Keep it dumb: regex over user turns, no LLM call (this runs in the passive suite; free and deterministic beats clever). False positives are cheap - candidates go to triage, not to tickets/open/. Do NOT auto-file tickets from signals; the miner emits candidates only, per the passive-skill read-only contract (writing to analysis/candidate_tickets.jsonl is its existing sanctioned output).
